title: Patient Assessment Simulator
description: An interactive clinical scenario tool that walks nursing students through head-to-toe patient assessments. Students make clinical decisions at branching points and receive immediate feedback on their reasoning.
author: Dr. Maria Chen

title: Faculty AI Evaluation Toolkit
description: A comprehensive recommendation engine that suggests AI tools and pedagogical activities based on discipline, Bloom's taxonomy level, task type, and audience. Features 500+ activities across 33 vetted AI tools with full source provenance.
author: Jake (Lewis University Library)

title: Ethical Dilemma Discussion Engine
description: Presents students with real-world ethical scenarios and facilitates structured moral reasoning using multiple ethical frameworks (utilitarian, deontological, virtue ethics, care ethics). Generates discussion prompts for small group work.
author: Dr. Sarah Whitfield

title: METAR/TAF Weather Decoder
description: Translates coded aviation weather reports (METARs and TAFs) into plain language with visual annotations. Includes a quiz mode where students decode real-world weather observations for flight planning practice.
author: Capt. James Rivera

title: Biology Lab Safety Prompt Library
description: A curated collection of 40+ reusable prompts for generating lab safety scenarios, pre-lab quizzes, and hazard identification exercises using generative AI. Includes instructor notes on how to adapt each prompt for different course levels.
author: Dr. Kenji Nakamura

title: Plain-Language Library Policy for Responsible AI Use
description: A model institutional policy document for responsible AI use in academic library contexts, covering video creation, content generation, and patron-facing services. Written in accessible language with implementation guidance for library administrators.
author: Jake (Lewis University Library)

title: Human-Centered AI Writing Workshop
description: A three-session lesson plan sequence for first-year composition courses that teaches students to use AI as a collaborative writing partner while maintaining their own voice. Includes session outlines, discussion questions, sample assignments, and a reflective rubric for assessing human-AI co-authored work.
author: Prof. Andrea Simmons

title: Case Law Pattern Analyzer
description: Presents landmark criminal justice cases and helps students identify legal reasoning patterns, precedent chains, and jurisdictional variations. Includes a comparison mode for analyzing how similar fact patterns produce different outcomes.
author: Prof. Marcus Webb

title: Regression Analysis Sandbox
description: A hands-on tool where students upload or generate datasets, fit linear and polynomial regression models, and explore residual plots. Visualizes the impact of outliers and influential points on model fit.
author: Dr. Lin Zhao

title: AI-Assisted Clinical Documentation Rubric
description: An assessment rubric and faculty guide for evaluating student clinical notes written with AI support. Distinguishes between appropriate AI collaboration (grammar, formatting, terminology lookup) and inappropriate reliance (clinical judgment, patient-specific reasoning). Includes sample scored work and calibration exercises.
author: Dr. Maria Chen

title: AI-Assisted Explainer Video Production Workflow
description: A documented workflow and template set for creating short educational explainer videos using AI tools for scriptwriting (Claude), voiceover (ElevenLabs), visuals (Midjourney), and editing assembly. Includes three completed example videos on information literacy topics with full production notes.
author: Jake (Lewis University Library)

title: Business Ethics Case Study Generator Framework
description: A structured prompt framework and instructor guide for using AI to generate discipline-relevant ethical case studies for business courses. Includes a taxonomy of ethical dilemma types, a prompt template with customizable parameters (industry, stakeholders, complexity), and a facilitation guide for classroom discussion.
author: Dr. Patricia Okonkwo
